record_identity.title: Bounded runtime transparency and non-omniscient self-explanation coverage

summary: Records the pre-existing CAM/Caelestis coverage for FM-0030. Annex L already requires execution-state distinction, confidence calibration, non-omniscience, and an audit surface sufficient to distinguish known action state from unavailable runtime cause. The VIGIL record supplies the failure-specific crosswalk; it does not amend VIGIL or claim vendor adoption.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: A responding intelligence should provide bounded status at the highest level it can truthfully attest to. A visible interruption may be reported as an interruption; a known safety or runtime review may be disclosed when that state is actually available; unavailable diagnostics must not be replaced by plausible-sounding self-narration.

change_classification.change_scope: Caelestis Architecture / Annex L execution-state, confidence, audit-surface, and non-omniscience doctrine
change_classification.implementation_level: pre-existing canonical doctrine; VIGIL crosswalk only

changed_components: No CAM instrument was amended in this retrospective record.
Existing Annex L §2.5 Execution-State Claim coverage identified.
Existing Annex L §5 confidence-calibration coverage identified.
Existing Annex L §5.4.7 action-pathway and audit-surface coverage identified.
Existing Annex L §8 Non-Omniscience coverage identified.
change_details.change_summary: The CAM repair content is the pre-existing requirement to distinguish capability, intention, authorisation, attempt, success, failure, blockage, simulation, and unavailable cause; signal uncertainty; preserve a sufficient audit surface; and avoid implying knowledge beyond the responding intelligence's visibility.
change_details.doctrine_change: No Caelestis doctrine was changed. Existing canonical provisions were identified and linked to FM-0030.
change_details.literal_implementation_location: See corpus_implementation.entries for exact wording, prior-text status, commit, path, and direct source link.
change_details.implemented_or_recorded_change: Pre-existing control relied upon—no corpus amendment. This PATCH records the decision to rely on the quoted Caelestis clauses.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Make the CAM rule for bounded runtime self-explanation discoverable from the ecosystem failure record.
impact_summary.known_limitations: This patch note does not establish that any vendor runtime has adopted or conforms to the CAM provisions.

remaining_work: Continue named-runtime monitoring for interruption narration, execution-state accuracy, and diagnostic visibility.

cam_internal.governance_layer: epistemic integrity / execution-state narration / bounded runtime transparency
cam_internal.routing_note: PATCH routing records only the Caelestis instruments amended by this decision. The relied-upon instruments are pre-existing controls, not amendments.

repair_provenance.repair_basis: Pre-existing control relied upon—no corpus amendment. Existing canonical Caelestis coverage was identified, verified, and linked without amending doctrine.

coverage_origin.effective_date: pre-existing by 2026-07-14; verified at cited historical commit

record_reconstruction.reason: Legacy PATCH reconstruction: the earlier record preserved narrative and provenance but did not expose literal, commit-addressed corpus implementation text.
record_reconstruction.method: Direct Git-object comparison of the VIGIL record, cited Caelestis implementation history, canonical file paths, section boundaries, prior wording where recoverable, and current main state.
limitations: The legacy narrative and evidence fields were preserved; record_reconstruction and the 2026-07-24 interpretive review disclose this later structural repair.
External vendor adoption and runtime conformance were not inferred from Caelestis corpus evidence.
The cited wording is the historical canonical control used by the decision; later corpus revisions may supersede it.
